evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix clearly shown; diagonal annotated in white on viridis; off-diagonal in Blues; rows/cols labeled C1-C10; claim is unambiguous.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set in script; fonts are Type 3 (outline/bitmap) in the PDF (DejaVuSans), not editable Type 42; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blues for off-diagonal and viridis for diagonal are both perceptually uniform and colorblind-safe; however only a single colorbar for off-diagonal is shown—diagonal scale is implicit.
evidence.layout_density: 7x6 inch figure with tight_layout and bbox_inches; panels well-filled; no gaping whitespace; colorbar present; all cell annotations readable at fontsize 8-9.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; no legend overlap issues; but missing a second colorbar for the diagonal viridis scale, which Reviewer 2 would likely flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF saved via Matplotlib backend but fonts are Type 3 (not Type 42); text is not selectable/editable in standard PDF viewers; no rcParams fonttype=42 in script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (26 KB), figure.png (59 KB), caption.tex (344 B), gen_figure.py (2.5 KB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains rows=true class, columns=predicted; states dual colormap usage; provides specific numerical range claim (69% C1 to 92% C8).

overall_strengths: Clear scientific communication: dual-colormap design cleanly separates diagonal (viridis) from off-diagonal (Blues), with adaptive text color for readability.
Caption is specific and informative, providing a concrete quantitative claim (per-class accuracy range 69%-92%) and accurately describing figure elements.
overall_weaknesses: Typography/vector fidelity: fonttype=42 not set, resulting in Type 3 (non-editable) fonts in the PDF and default DejaVu Sans family instead of a publication-quality typeface.
Diagonal viridis color scale has no colorbar, so the exact correct-classification counts are only available via cell annotations, not from a legend—Reviewer 2 would likely request one.
